mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month when the policy rate is still being cut rather than held steady.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average after monthly FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound implies rising real policy accommodation and inflation surprise risk, which compresses equity multiples and weakens S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 3.0% while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50%
regime_hint: early inflation rebound with policy still anchored
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the return difference versus untriggered periods is statistically indistinguishable for 3 consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises quickly, payroll income and household confidence deteriorate, which feeds through to weaker earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if monthly labor-slowdown triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates broad retail demand strength, supporting revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when sales growth is not offset by labor weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly RSAFS growth fails to coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the average forward return flips negative in a rolling 24-month evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO after a contraction reflects restored industrial throughput and tighter earnings dispersion, which supports equity prices over the next quarter as cyclical risk premia compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after having been negative for at least 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if industrial recovery triggers are not followed by higher 60-day SPX returns than the unconditional mean, or if the effect is absent across two consecutive business-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS signals a broad demand slowdown that reduces future sales and housing-related spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in housing starts and retail sales does not improve downside prediction for 20-day SPX returns versus single-series models, or if triggered joint declines are followed by positive returns more than half the time.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate with continuing job gains indicates a tight labor market that supports wage income and consumption, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in expansionary regimes.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 6.0% and PAYEMS is rising over the latest month
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment expansion signals do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the interaction term adds no predictive lift over independent UNRATE and PAYEMS effects in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, an increase in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold lowers equity duration value and tightens credit conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.00% and CPIAUCSL inflation is not falling on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-trigger episodes do not show negative 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the signal becomes positive during tightening cycles in any sustained 1-year window.
